title: Panic symptoms follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports structured adult panic-disorder diagnosis, history, comorbidity review, panic presentations, and proportionate investigation to exclude acute physical problems before primary-care follow-up.
examination.rationale: Supports panic presentation review and exclusion of acute physical problems for panic symptoms follow-up; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports structured adult panic-disorder diagnosis, history, comorbidity review, panic presentations, and proportionate investigation to exclude acute physical problems before primary-care follow-up.

source.title: Generalised anxiety disorder and panic disorder in adults: management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.5 — diagnosis, presentation, and exclusion of acute physical problems

referral.text: Supports clinician-documented discussion of psychological and medication options, patient preference, information, monitoring, self-help, referral, and follow-up without autonomous treatment output or doses.
referral.rationale: Supports clinician-led treatment review and shared decisions for panic symptoms follow-up; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented discussion of psychological and medication options, patient preference, information, monitoring, self-help, referral, and follow-up without autonomous treatment output or doses.

source.title: Generalised anxiety disorder and panic disorder in adults: management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.6–1.3.25 — clinician-led treatment and shared decisions

referral.text: Supports reassessment of prior treatment and concordance, substance use, comorbidity, function, social support, stressors, avoidance, comprehensive risk, side effects, efficacy, and a shared care plan.
referral.rationale: Supports reassessment, risk and specialist-care context for panic symptoms follow-up; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports reassessment of prior treatment and concordance, substance use, comorbidity, function, social support, stressors, avoidance, comprehensive risk, side effects, efficacy, and a shared care plan.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Episode course, triggers, physical symptoms, avoidance, function, adherence, adverse effects, physical assessment, mental state, risk and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for panic symptoms follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
